Japanese: 同日　某時刻 ビタミン広場
English: January 7 Vitamin Square

Japanese: ‥‥あれ。 オジさん、いないよ。
English: Hmm... I don't see any sign of Mr. Kudo. Do you?

Japanese: マメを買いに行ってるのかも しれないね。
English: Maybe he went to buy another ton of bird seeds.

Japanese: なんにしても、あまり 会いたくないな、今は。
English: I was kind of hoping he wouldn't be here, anyway. At least, not for now.

Japanese: （もう、今日はじゅうぶん 食らわされたからな。マメなら）
English: (Besides, any more seeds today and I'm liable to turn into a real phoenix!)

Japanese: フルーティーだよね。 なんていうか。
English: This place is so fruity!

Japanese: ‥‥まあ、いいんじゃないかな。 ぼくもリンゴは大好きだし。
English: That's not a bad thing. Besides, I love apples. They're among my favorites.

Japanese: あ！　じゃあ、あの すべり台、もってこいじゃない！
English: Then that apple slide is perfect for you!

Japanese: ‥‥なにがどう “もってこい”なんだよ。
English: And what is so "perfect" about it?

Japanese: はいはい、なるほどくん。 すべってすべって。
English: Oh come on. Don't be a stick in the mud! Slide down it a few times. Go on! Woo!

Japanese: や、やだよ。
English: No way!

Japanese: すべったら、かならず砂まみれに なるシステムだぞ、あれ。
English: I'd get covered in sand if I slid down that slide. Anyone can see that.

Japanese: ううん、たしかに。 それがなきゃ、あたしもねえ‥‥
English: Yeah, you're right. Otherwise, I'd give it a try, too.

Japanese: ハトだ。
English: Hey, look. Pigeons!

Japanese: たくさんいるね。
English: Yeah. And heaps of them, too.

Japanese: ね。知ってる？　ハトって、 平和のシンボルなんだよ！
English: Did you know that pigeons are a symbol of peace?

Japanese: そりゃ、白いハトだけだろ。
English: That's a dove, not a pigeon.

Japanese: かわいそうだね、この子たち。
English: Poor things.

Japanese: 色が白くないだけなのに‥‥ 平和になりそこねちゃって。
English: Just because they're gray means they can't be symbols of peace and harmony?

Japanese: な、何もそこまで しんみりしなくても‥‥
English: You're over-thinking this one by just a smidge, Maya.

Japanese: いやあ、砂場には 燃えたねー、あたしも。
English: I used to love sandboxes like you would not believe.

Japanese: 燃えた？　砂場遊びに？
English: Really? You?

Japanese: ホラ。じしゃくを使って 砂鉄あつめ。
English: Sure. Finding iron filings in the sand with a magnet was my favorite thing to do.

Japanese: サテツ‥‥地味だなあ。
English: Iron filings? Wow... That's too exciting for words.

Japanese: 『砂場じゅうの砂鉄を 集めきる！』って決意して。
English: It was my ambition to collect every single shred of iron in the sandbox.

Japanese: あたしも若かったな。 あのころは。
English: I was such a kid back then!

Japanese: ‥‥で、集めきったの？
English: So, did you manage to get all the iron?

Japanese: ううん。 もう少しだったんだけどねえ。
English: No, I never did. I think I came close, though.

Japanese: あ。そういえば、まだ とってあるよ！　そのときの砂鉄。
English: Come to think of it, I still have all the iron filings I found way back when.

Japanese: いる？
English: You want them?

Japanese: いらない。
English: No.

Japanese: あのさ。このみかん見てたら 思い出したんだけど。
English: Looking at this orange reminds me...

Japanese: ‥‥なに？
English: Of what?

Japanese: やっぱり、お正月といえば、 おもち食べながらみかん食べて、
English: That you're supposed to eat a lot of them to ward off colds in the winter.

Japanese: おせちをつつきながらテレビ見て、 さらにトランプでババ抜きだよね。
English: You can't have fun during the holidays if you're sick in bed, you know.

Japanese: マコちゃん助けたら、 みんなでやろうか。
English: Speaking of fun, if we get Maggey acquitted, maybe we can all get together sometime.

Japanese: でもマコちゃん、ジャンケンすら 勝ったことがない、って‥‥。
English: OK! ...But what do we play with someone who's never even won a game of Tic-Tac-Toe?

Japanese: ‥‥じゃ、ルールを変えよう。
English: How about we change the rules a bit, then? We could play Old Maid, and... um...

Japanese: 最後にババ持ってたら勝ち、 ってコトに。
English: We could say the person who's left with the old maid at the end is the winner. How's that?

Japanese: ‥‥《ババ引き》かあ。 いいね、それ！
English: Not bad... Not bad at all! We could even rename the game to "Fair Maiden"!

Japanese: あれ。これって‥‥
English: Hey, check this out...

Japanese: 近よらないほうがいいよ。 ものすごいコトになるから。
English: I wouldn't get too close to that if I were you. Otherwise you might be in for a shock.

Japanese: （どうやら‥‥近くに ひそんでるらしいな‥‥）
English: My phony must be lurking someplace nearby...

Japanese: （‥‥都会に放たれた トラがッ！）
English: Just imagine! A Tiger, loose in the city!

Japanese: ホンモノは、都会に捨てられた 子ネコみたいなのにねえ。
English: Meanwhile the real Phoenix is like an abandoned chick, lost in a vast urban jungle!
Huh?

Japanese: なるほどくんもがんばって、 早くトラになるんだよ。
English: Don't worry. Someday, you'll grow up and become a ferocious tiger, too! Don't lose hope!

Japanese: （ニセモノを目指すハメになった）
English: (Why is she trying to pep-talk me into becoming my phony...?)

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 ビタミン広場
English: January 7 Vitamin Square

Japanese: 出たッ！　マメのオジさんだ！ ハトにエサやってるよ！
English: There he is! Old Seedy's back feeding the pigeons again!

Japanese: 食らえッ！　食らって飛べッ！
English: There! Take this! And this! And get out of my park!

Japanese: やっぱり、荒れてるな‥‥。
English: Like I thought, he's really mad...

Japanese: 下を向いて、目を合わさないように コッソリ帰ろう。
English: Come on, Maya. Just keep your head down and let's sneak away while we still can...

Japanese: え。どうして？ おーい、オジさーん！
English: What? Why? Hellooo, old man!

Japanese: あ。ま、真宵ちゃん‥‥
English: What are you doing, Maya!?

Japanese: ムッ！　‥‥‥‥プイッ。
English: Huh!? ...Kah!

Japanese: あれ。 そっぽ向いちゃった。
English: Hey, he just turned his back on us!

Japanese: そりゃまあ。オジさんのプライド、 まるつぶれだからなあ。
English: I'm not surprised. I bet I really hurt his pride in court this morning.

Japanese: ね、オジさんオジさん。
English: Hey, Mr. Kudo!

Japanese: ぽっぽっぽお！　ハトぽっぽお ‥‥‥‥とぉ！
English: Hmm, ha-hmm, hmm... Hmm, pi-pi-pigeon, hmm... Kah!

Japanese: お話、聞かせてくださいよー。
English: Look, we really need to talk to you, alright?

Japanese: オニはーそとォ！ 福はーうちィ！
English: Out with the demons! In with good fortune!

Japanese: いたたたたたたたたた。 ‥‥あたしもマメ投げられた。
English: Ow! Ow! Ow! Seeds... Shell splinters... Painful...

Japanese: やれやれ。オニあつかいか‥‥。
English: I always knew you were a demon, Maya!

Japanese: ど、どうして今日の法廷で 話してくれなかったんですか！
English: Why didn't you mention this in court this morning?

Japanese: 話そうと思ったワイ！ ‥‥だのに‥‥
English: Well, I would have if you'd given me the chance!

Japanese: アンタらがよってたかって、 ワシを放り出したではないかッ！
English: But you all bullied me out of the courtroom!

Japanese: ‥‥はあ。 証人。ごくろうさまでした。
English: *sigh* Thank you, Mr. Kudo. You've certainly earned your kudos for today.

Japanese: ままま、待っていただきたい！ そ、そういうことであれば、その。
English: WAIT! WAIT A MINUTE! If that's the case, there's more! I've got more to say!

Japanese: ‥‥あ、そうだ！ なんなら、あの話を‥‥
English: Oh, yes! I remember something else...

Japanese: 法廷係官！ この証人をつまみ出しなさい。
English: Bailiff! Escort the witness out of the courtroom!

Japanese: ワルいの、ワシじゃないモン！ アンタらだモン！
English: It's not my fault! You're the ones to blame!

Japanese: ‥‥先にこっちを話してくれれば よかったのに‥‥
English: You could have at least told us before we got to court.

Japanese: そんなにダイジなコトなの？ オジさんが電話したこと。
English: Is it really that important that Mr. Kudo was the one who called the police...?

Japanese: （警察が到着するまでの、 空白の時間‥‥）
English: (What's important is the unaccounted time before the police arrived.)

Japanese: （店内には、被害者と、 キゼツしたマコちゃんと‥‥）
English: (The victim was dead, and Maggey was unconscious.)

Japanese: （彼女‥‥いや、彼だけが 残されていたコトになる！）
English: (Which leaves that woman... I mean, that man, alone in the restaurant!)

Japanese: オジさんは‥‥追い払われたの かもしれない。
English: Mr. Kudo might have been chased out of the place on purpose.

Japanese: “追い払われた”‥‥？
English: What do you mean?

Japanese: 警察が来るまで、 店にいてもらいたくなかった‥‥
English: Maybe a certain someone didn't want him in the restaurant.
Ah!

Japanese: ああ、そうさ！　どうせワシは ジャマモノだともさ！
English: Oh, sure! You go ahead and say I was in the way as usual!

Japanese: ワシなんか、ハトにまみれて マメ食ってりゃいいのさ！
English: I suppose I should've been getting myself covered in pigeon poop instead, hmm?

Japanese: （とにかく、ハナシを聞いた ほうがいいな‥‥）
English: (We need to get more details about what exactly happened...)

Japanese: （マコちゃんと‥‥ あの、店長に！）
English: (...from Maggey... and from Mr. Armstrong!)

Japanese: あの。‥‥今日の法廷は、 すみませんでした。
English: Um... I'm sorry about what happened in court earlier today.

Japanese: フン！　ケーベツするがいいさ！ どうせワシは、アレさ！
English: Kah! Everyone'll be talking about me behind my back now!

Japanese: 女給さんのおシリに見とれて ロクにカオもおぼえていない、
English: A dirty old man who was so busy looking at the serving girl's backside...

Japanese: すけべいなクソ野郎なのさ！ フケツでおぞましい初老の怪物さ！
English: ...that he can't remember her face! A filthy, depraved animal!

Japanese: い、いや、そんな‥‥
English: N-Not at all!

Japanese: いいか！　アンタがなんと言おうと あの女給は入れたんだよ！
English: Are you listening to me, boy!? I don't care what you say, I saw that waitress put it in!

Japanese: ワカゾウのコーシーに、 なんぞ、白い粉をッ！
English: She put some white powder into the young lad's javaccino!

Japanese: そうなんですか‥‥
English: We hear you...

Japanese: それに！　あのワカゾウは、 イヤホンをブチこんでたんだよ！
English: And another thing! The young layabout was wearing an earpiece!

Japanese: イカれたメガネをかけた耳にッ！
English: On the same side as the lens of his broken spectacles!

Japanese: うう‥‥ゴメンなさい‥‥。
English: W-We're really sorry...

Japanese: たしかに、花ビンのコトはアレさ。 スットコチョイだったさ！
English: So I made a little mistake about the vase. So what!?

Japanese: でも‥‥ワシは‥‥ワシはぁぁ！
English: I know what I saw! I tell you, I tell you, I tell you!

Japanese: ま、まあまあ、落ちついて‥‥。
English: OK, OK! Take it easy, please, Mr. Kudo.

Japanese: うるさいワ！　これでも食らえィ！
English: Don't tell me to take it easy, you spikey-haired brat! Take this!

Japanese: あの‥‥。オジさんて、 職人さんなんですよね！
English: Um... You said you were a craftsman, right?

Japanese: フン！　このセチがらい世の中、 ワシの居場所なんか、ないワ！
English: Kah! The modern world casts honest craftsmen like me aside in droves!

Japanese: そ、そんなコトは‥‥
English: S-Surely it's not that...

Japanese: ワシの家は代々、職人だった！ そりゃもう、江戸時代からだ！
English: I come from a long line of craftsmen. Right back to the time of the shoguns!

Japanese: ワカるか！　生まれた瞬間から 紋章上絵師だった、ワシのキモチ。
English: Do you hear me!? I didn't become an embroiderer, I was BORN one!

Japanese: ‥‥あたしも似たようなモン なんだけどな。
English: Actually... I'm kind of in the same situation myself.

Japanese: ワシだって‥‥ワシだって‥‥ I... I...
冷や汗でグズグズになったジイサン のハナっツラに指をこすりつけて、
English: I wanted to stick my fingers up that dribbling old judge's nose...

Japanese: 耳もとで“異議あり！”と 叫びたおしてみたかったワ！
English: ...and scream right down his earhole... "Objection!"

Japanese: ‥‥オジさん、弁護士に なりたかったんだね‥‥。
English: Oh... So, did you want to become a lawyer when you were young...?

Japanese: どうでもいいけど、 グチっぽくなってきたな。
English: I don't think that's quite it, Maya. I think he's just in a bad mood, that's all...

Japanese: ワシのグチは、ツナミのごとく 押しよせ、さかまき、飲みこむぞ！
English: I've got a tsunami of frustration inside and it's ready to burst out!

Japanese: （‥‥どうしよう。 長くなりそうだな。‥‥グチ）
English: (If we let him start rambling now, we might never shut him up! What should I do?)

Japanese: あの。ちょっと今はアレなので‥‥ 今度、ゆっくり聞かせてください。
English: Um, actually, we've got places to go, things to check and stuff... Maybe another time?

Japanese: よおし！　ヤクソクだぞ！ かならず聞きに来るんだからな！
English: Fine! Whatever! Just don't forget! I've got a tsunami's worth of grumbling to do.

Japanese: は、はい。 この子が楽しみにしてますから。
English: Yes, yes. This girl right here is really looking forward to hearing it all. Honest!

Japanese: なんであたしが！
English: What!? I never said that!

Japanese: ワシの店で待っとるからな！ ‥‥持ってけ、このワカゾウがッ！
English: Come to the place where I work, then. Here. Bring this along with you.

Japanese: （マメにまじって、ハンバーガーの 割引券がとんできた）
English: (What is this? It's covered in seeds... A discount coupon for a burger!?)

Japanese: よっぽど聞いてほしいんだね。 ‥‥グチ。
English: He really wants someone to grumble at, huh? ...Although, it IS a burger joint...

Japanese: （とりあえず、 聞いてみるか‥‥グチ）
English: (Guess I better let him talk...)

Japanese: お仕事は、ないんですか？ 職人さんの。
English: So, there's not much call for craftsmen these days, then?

Japanese: あたりまえだ！　ワシなんぞ、 使いっ走りがお似あいなのさ！
English: Of course not, you idiot! All I'm good for nowadays is running errands!

Japanese: “使いっ走り”‥‥？
English: Errands?

Japanese: ダレもカレも、 年寄りをコキ使いおって！
English: Everyone takes advantage of the elderly!

Japanese: “ジイちゃん、とうふ買ってきて” “イヌのさんぽ、おねがい”
English: "Buy some bread, gramps!" "Take the dog for a walk, granddad!"

Japanese: “ハトにエサ、やってきて”‥‥ ワシは町の便利屋さんかいッ！
English: "Feed the pigeons, old man!" What am I? Some sort of two- bit community handyman!?

Japanese: はああ‥‥。
English: Um, well...

Japanese: “とうふ買ってきて”は リカイできる。しかし‥‥
English: "Buy some bread", now that I can understand.

Japanese: “ハトにエサやってきて” ‥‥これは、なんだ！
English: But what's the point of feeding some seedy pigeons!?

Japanese: アキラカに“どこか行ってきて” ってコトじゃないかッ！
English: Why don't people say what they mean!? "Get lost!" That's what they're trying to say!

Japanese: ああ、そうさ！ ワシはジャマモノなのさ！
English: Oh, yes! I'm just an inconvenience, you see!

Japanese: ‥‥ウチでも、メシ屋でもな！
English: At home... At that restaurant... I just get in the way, don't I!?

Japanese: そ、そんなことは‥‥
English: I-I'm sure you don't.

Japanese: （ちょっと待てよ‥‥。 今、なんて言った‥‥？）
English: (Wait a minute! What did he just say?)

Japanese: （ウチでも‥‥ “メシ屋でも”‥‥？）
English: (At home and... at that restaurant?)

Japanese: メシ屋‥‥‥吐麗美庵で、 たのまれたコトがあるんですか？
English: Hold up... By "restaurant", are you talking about Trés Bien?

Japanese: その‥‥“使いっ走り”を。
English: Did you get asked to run an errand there, too?

Japanese: ああ、そうともさ！　あの ワカゾウが、ぶっ倒れた日にな！
English: Yes, I did! The very day that young brat was poisoned.

Japanese: な‥‥ なんですってェェェッ！
English: WH-WHAT!?

Japanese: 事件があった日‥‥吐麗美庵で 何をたのまれたんですか？
English: So on the day of the incident, what were you asked to do?

Japanese: おお！　よくぞ 聞いてくれたワ！
English: Glad you asked, boy! Because I'll tell you what I was asked to do!

Japanese: ‥‥ワカゾウがぶっ倒れて、
English: All of a sudden that young lad slumped over the table...

Japanese: ‥‥女給さんもぶっ倒れて、
English: The serving girl collapsed...

Japanese: ワシは花ビンをぶっ倒した。 アタマん中、まっ白だよ。もう。
English: And I broke that vase. It all happened so fast, I was in a bit of a daze, you see.

Japanese: そこに、あの店長がワシに 叫んだのさ。
English: Then the owner shouted over to me.

Japanese: “すみません！　そこのヒト！ ケーサツを呼んでくださいな！”
English: "Excusez-moi! You! Call la police!"

Japanese: 自分で呼べよ、ケーサツぐらい！ ‥‥と、今なら言える。
English: "Call them yourself!" I should have said back. But I didn't think of it at the time.

Japanese: それで‥‥呼んだんですか？ ケーサツ。
English: So, did you end up calling the police?

Japanese: まあ‥‥とにかくワシ、 アタマん中、まっ白だったから。
English: Like I said, I was in a bit of a daze.

Japanese: 警察への通報は‥‥ 携帯電話を使ったんですか？
English: Did you call them on your cell phone?

Japanese: そんなハイカラなモノ、 持ってるワケがなかろうッ！
English: Kah! Do I look like I'd have one of those new-fangled thingamajigs?

Japanese: 店のそとで、公衆電話を 探したワ、モチロン。
English: I went out looking for a pay phone, of course!
You went looking for one...?

Japanese: なかなか見つからなくてな。 ５分ぐらいムダにしちまった。
English: I couldn't find one right away, you know. Wandered around for 5 minutes or so.
F-Five minutes...?

Japanese: じゃあ、その５分間、 吐麗美庵には‥‥？
English: So for five minutes after the incident happened...

Japanese: あの店長が、ひとりで 見張っとったんだろうな。
English: Yessiree, the owner was at Trés Bien on his own.

Japanese: あの‥‥ちょっと、 見ていただきたいんですけど‥‥
English: Um, would you mind taking a look at this?
Kah!

Japanese: あの、すみません。
English: Excuse me...

Japanese: ワシのグチにトコトンつき合うなら ‥‥チラと見てやってもいい。
English: You let me get a million of my grumbles off my chest, and I'll take one quick look.

Japanese: ワシのグチは、大地震のごとく ゆらぎ、ふるえ、飲みこむぞ！
English: I've got an earthquake of frustration inside and it's ready to rumble!

Japanese: ‥‥やめとこう。
English: ...On second thought, forget it.

Japanese: “トコトン”で“チラ”じゃ ワリに合わない。
English: A million grumbles for one quick look doesn't sound like a good deal... for me.

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 カリヨーゼ
English: January 7 Tender Lender

Japanese: ‥‥なんか、フツウじゃない 空気がただよってるね。
English: ...This place gives off a really strange vibe, doesn't it?

Japanese: どうも、《ナニワのゼニトラ》は いないみたいだな。
English: Looks like "The Tiger" isn't in his lair.

Japanese: （‥‥ちょっと、 ホッとしてしまった）
English: (And that is, as they say, a very good thing.)

Japanese: ‥‥いらっしゃいませ‥‥
English: ...Welcome...

Japanese: うっ‥‥
English: *gulp*

Japanese: （‥‥この、聞いてるだけで 死にたくなる、暗ぁい声は‥‥）
English: (Talk about a creepy voice! It makes your soul want to shrivel up and die!)

Japanese: ‥‥ご融資の相談、ですよね‥‥
English: You're here... to discuss a loan...?

Japanese: え。いや、その‥‥。
English: Uh, no. Not exactly...

Japanese: 店長は、外出してますから‥‥ おとなしく、お待ちください‥‥
English: The manager is away at the moment... Wait quietly... please...

Japanese: 行っちゃった。
English: She's gone! Just like that!

Japanese: しかたないな。 また、あとで来るか。
English: I guess we'll just have to come back another time.

Japanese: ね。ね。なるほどくん。 今がチャンスだよ。
English: But this is the perfect opportunity, Nick!

Japanese: なんか、アヤしげなトコだし。 ちょっと調べてみようよ。
English: This place reeks of suspicion! Come on, Nick! Let's take a look around, OK?

Japanese: だ、だいじょうぶかな‥‥。
English: Do you think it'd be OK...?

Japanese: ヘーキだって。バレやしない‥‥
English: Of course! No one will ever know...
...Coffee...?

Japanese: ひゃひゃああああっ！
English: Aaaaaaaah!

Japanese: ここに、置いておきますから‥‥
English: I'll leave it here for you to enjoy... Quietly.

Japanese: はは、ハイッ！ ど、ど、どうも。
English: Y-Yes! Thank you!

Japanese: ‥‥机には さわらないでくださいね‥‥
English: Do not touch the desk... Please...

Japanese: なな、なるほどくん！ かかか、帰ろう！
English: N-N-Nick! Let's g-get out of here!

Japanese: （やれやれ‥‥）
English: (NOW she wants to get out of here...)

Japanese: コォラァアアアアアアアアアアアア アアアアアアアアアアアアアアアア
English: Gwoaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa

English: Whad are youse two snoopin' around in my office for!?

English: N-Nothing. W-We were just...

Japanese: ワイのお宝ジュータンがコレ、 ハイまみれになっとるやんけェッ！

English: I-It wasn't us! It was already like th--

English: Youse wanna argue with me!? Is that what you' doin'? You dink youse can take me on!?

Japanese: ボテくりこかしまわしてさらにコレ シバきたおしたろかいワレェェッ！
English: I'm gonna flatten youse two into pancakes and turn youse into my new rugs!

Japanese: きゃわわわわああああああッ！
English: Aaaaaaaah!

Japanese: ‥‥帰っていらしたの‥‥ トラさま‥‥
English: Don Tigre... You're back...

Japanese: （‥‥この、地の底から聞こえる、 うらみがましい声は‥‥）
English: (Ack... That voice... It's like evil seeping into your head through your ears...)

Japanese: ごめんなさい‥‥トラさま‥‥
English: ...I'm sorry... Don Tigre...

Japanese: ‥‥そのハイザラ、わたしが ひっくり返しましたの‥‥
English: I knocked over that ashtray before... and...

Japanese: （うわあ‥‥イノチ知らずだな。 ドナり倒されるぞ‥‥）
English: (Eek! Has she got a death wish, or what!?)

Japanese: あ‥‥そ、そうなんや。
English: Oh, right.

Japanese: え。
English: Huh?

Japanese: う、うらみちゃんなら‥‥ま。 しゃアないな。うん。しゃアない。
English: F-Forget about it, Violetta. I-It's noddin'...

Japanese: （なな、なんだなんだなんだ！）
English: (What, what, what!?)

Japanese: カワイイうらみちゃんのコトや。 なんでも。ゆ、ユルしたるでェ！
English: I ain't gonna get mad at you. You' too cute, you hear!

Japanese: （‥‥不公平だ！）
English: (That's so unfair!)

Japanese: ‥‥わたし、ビスケットを焼いて 待ってましたの‥‥
English: Here... Have some cookies... I just baked them...

Japanese: 融資の話をしながら、 濃厚なお茶をいただきましょう‥‥
English: And you'll need some strong espresso... while you're discussing your loan...

Japanese: コラァアアアアアッ！ 成歩堂　龍一ィィッ！
English: Gwoaaaaaaaaaaaaaar! Phoenix Wright!

Japanese: ‥‥は、はい！
English: Y-Yes!?

Japanese: やるのォ‥‥。ついに ここまで、たどり着いたか‥‥
English: Youse either crazy or just plain stupid to chase after me!

Japanese: ゴクロウなコトや。わざわざ、 ホンモノが出てくるとはなァ‥‥
English: I worked so hard, but now youse gotta come and mess up my plan...

Japanese: （ぜ、ゼニトラ‥‥ やっぱり、コイツが‥‥）
English: (So it was him... He's my phony...)

Japanese: まあ‥‥。何度やっても アンタの負けは、動かへんでェ。
English: Heh, but I don't care. No one gets in my way!

Japanese: な、なんだと？ ‥‥いや、なんですって？
English: Wh-What? I mean, e-excuse me?

Japanese: クククク‥‥あんなコムスメ、 ほっておけばいいものを‥‥。
English: Heh heh heh! Youse shoulda left de little girl at home, Wright.

Japanese: あの‥‥ 聞きたいことがあるんですけども
English: Um... I have a few things I want to ask--

Japanese: おらァアアアアアアァァァァァッ！
English: Gwoaaaaaaaaaaaaaar!

Japanese: きゃわわわわああああああッ！
English: Aaaaaaaah!

Japanese: ワイには聞きたいコトなんかない！ もう、会うこともないで！
English: No questions! Dis is de last time we meet!

Japanese: あ！　待てッ！ ‥‥いや、待ってください！
English: Ack! Wait! P-Please!

Japanese: ‥‥いやー、情けないねー なるほどくん。
English: ...That was pretty weak, Nick.

Japanese: 声が届かなくなるまで待ってから “待てッ！”だもんね。
English: You waited until he was out of earshot before you shouted after him.

Japanese: （真宵ちゃんに 言われたくないぞ‥‥）
English: (Like you're one to talk! I didn't hear you scream, "Hold it!" either!)
...The espresso...

Japanese: きゃはああああっ！
English: Aaaaaaaah!

Japanese: ‥‥さらに、ビスケット‥‥ お持ちしました‥‥
English: ...And cookies...

Japanese: （‥‥ホント、シンゾウに 悪いな、このヒト‥‥）
English: (This woman is definitely not good for my heart.)

Japanese: （“うらみちゃん”とか 呼ばれてたっけ‥‥）
English: (Now, what was it The Tiger called her...? "Violetta"?)

Japanese: なにこれ。
English: What's this?

Japanese: サンドバッグだよ。
English: It's a punching bag.

Japanese: えー！　あたしはヤだな。 こんなバッグ、持ち歩くの。
English: What!? No way. You wouldn't catch me walking around with a bag like that!

Japanese: ‥‥“持ち歩く”？
English: What do you mean, "walking around"?

Japanese: デザインはカッコわるいし、 だいいち、重すぎて持てないよ。
English: The design's gross to start with, and it's way too heavy to be practical.

Japanese: “サンド”っていうワリに、 何も挟んでないしねえ‥‥
English: And why's it called a "punching"-bag? Don't people know messenger bags are in!

Japanese: やっぱりあたしは、パリっぽく キメてみたいな。
English: I knew it. I was right before, back at Trés Bien. Paris fashion is more my thing.

Japanese: （どうやら、《ハンドバッグ》の 仲間だと思ってるらしいな‥‥）
English: (...I really, really hope she's pulling my chain on this one.)

Japanese: “三日坊主”‥‥か。
English: "Win Through Compromise"...?

Japanese: あたしのコトじゃないよ。
English: I wonder what that's supposed to mean...

Japanese: なんでこんなコトバを わざわざ、かざってるのかな。
English: It must mean something if they took the trouble to frame it like that.

Japanese: さあねえ。‥‥あたしのコトじゃ ないのはたしかだけど。
English: Yeah well, it still doesn't make any sense to me...
...That...

Japanese: きゃはわわあああっ！
English: Aaaaaaaah!

Japanese: 《カリヨーゼ》の 経営理念なんです‥‥
English: That's Tender Lender's guiding principle.

Japanese: けいえいりねん‥‥？
English: Oh?

Japanese: 『一度つかまえた客は、３日で 丸ボーズにムシるべし』‥‥
English: "Compromise the customer to win."

Japanese: ど、どうかな？　なるほどくん。
English: O-Oh, I see... How about you, Nick...?

Japanese: ぼ、ぼくは今の髪型が 気に入ってるから。
English: Yeah, umm... Well, as long as we don't have to compromise my hair, I'd say we're OK...

Japanese: （‥‥そんなスローガン、客に セツメイするなよ‥‥）
English: (That's one slogan no business owner should ever explain to their customers... *shudder*)

Japanese: まぶしい金のデスクに ＣＤプレイヤーが置いてある。
English: There's a CD player on the desk. But the desk is so loud, it's a wonder you can hear it.

Japanese: フタがあいてるよ。‥‥どんな 音楽を聞いてるのかな、ゼニトラ。
English: The lid's open. I wonder what kind of music The Tiger's into.

Japanese: ‥‥飲みましたか‥‥お茶‥‥
English: Have you finished... ...the coffee?

Japanese: ひゃひゃあああああっ！
English: Aaaaaaaah!

Japanese: え、ええ、飲みました。 ご、ごちそうさま。
English: Y-Yes, thanks. It was... lovely.

Japanese: ‥‥そうですか‥‥ 飲んじゃったんですか‥‥ククッ。
English: So... You drank it... all? Hee... Hee... Hee...
*gulp*

Japanese: ‥‥これ以上、何かさわったら‥‥ もう１ぱい、飲んでもらいます‥‥
English: ...If you touch anything else that doesn't belong to you... There's always another cup...

Japanese: （な‥‥ナニが入ってたんだ！ さっきのお茶！）
English: (Th-That coffee! I-It was laced with something! I'm almost sure of it!)

Japanese: なな、なるほどくん。 おなかがイタい！　イタいよお！
English: Nick! My stomach... It's killing me!

Japanese: 気のせいかな。
English: Oh, wait. Maybe it was just the burger I ate for breakfast...

Japanese: だ、だといいけど‥‥。
English: I-I sure hope so...

Japanese: （とにかく、今のうちに ＣＤを見ておくか‥‥）
English: (We better take a look at that CD while we're still alive and have the chance...)

Japanese: ああああああ‥‥ッ！
English: What the...!?

Japanese: な、なに？ やっぱり、ナニワ節‥‥？
English: What!? It's not the Rocko soundtrack, is it? Claw of the Tiger?

Japanese: このＣＤ‥‥どうやら、 サンプルらしいよ。
English: It's... It's a demo CD...

Japanese: サインペンでバンドの 名前が書いてある。
English: The artist's name has been hand-written on the disc in pen.

Japanese: 《クリーニング・ボンバー》 ‥‥って。
English: "MC Bomber"...

Japanese: ええええええッ！
English: WHAT!?

Japanese: （これが‥‥マコちゃんの 言っていたＣＤか‥‥！）
English: (This must be the CD Maggey told us about!)

Japanese: き、聞いてみようよ！　きっと、 “ろっけん・ろーる”だよ！
English: Let's listen to it! I bet it's heavy metal!

Japanese: ダメだよ！　お茶を飲まされる。
English: No way! That woman'll make us drink more coffee if we do!

Japanese: 《クリーニング・ボンバー》の データを法廷記録にファイルした。
English: MC Bomber added to the Court Record.

Japanese: うわー、ハイザラが ひっくり返ってるよ。
English: Oh, no. Someone's dropped the ashtray on the floor.

Japanese: これって、おそうじが タイヘンなんだよねー。
English: That's going to be a nightmare to clean up.

Japanese: たしかに‥‥ ジュータンの上だもんな。
English: Yeah. It's all over the rug and everything.

Japanese: あたしもムカシ、火バチを ひっくり返してさ。
English: I accidentally knocked over a really big space heater once. Cleaning up was such a pain.

Japanese: 泣きながらハイを 集めたっけ。
English: It was one of those super antiques where you have to burn a ton of charcoal.

Japanese: （火バチなんて、どうやったら ひっくり返せるんだ‥‥？）
English: (How did she manage to knock one of those over? Aren't they supposed to be super heavy?)

Japanese: ‥‥おっ！マッチが落ちてたよ。
English: Oh, hey! There's a book of matches here, too!

Japanese: へえ。今どき、紙マッチなんて めずらしいな‥‥‥‥‥‥？
English: Matches, huh? Places don't give those out much nowadays. ...Hey, wait a second!

Japanese: どうしたの？
English: What is it?

Japanese: この紙マッチ‥‥ 《吐麗美庵》って書いてある！
English: Look what's printed on the back! It says, "Trés Bien"!

Japanese: 《吐麗美庵のマッチ》のデータを 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Trés Bien Matches added to the Court Record.

Japanese: （このマッチ‥‥ 使えるかもしれないな‥‥）
English: (These matches could come in handy. We might be able to use them...)

Japanese: そうだね。‥‥事務所のコンロ、 なんか調子ワルいし。
English: Yeah. The pilot light for the office boiler keeps going out.

Japanese: ‥‥そういうコトじゃ ないんだ。
English: Swing and a miss, Maya. Swing and a miss.

Japanese: ダルマさんって、エラいよねー。
English: Let's see... This round doll thing is called a "daruma"... I think.

Japanese: 何度コロがしても、 ムクッて起きあがるんだもん。
English: I figured I'd read a book or two and be more cultured, in case you're wondering.

Japanese: こんなデカいダルマでも、 やっぱり起きるのかな。
English: You mean you aren't making stuff up for a change?

Japanese: ほおほお。それはキョーミ深い モンダイだねー。
English: Heh. I bet you also didn't know that no matter what, he'll always right himself.

Japanese: ほら。ほら。なるほどくん。 転がす転がす。
English: Go on, Nick! Give him a good shove!

Japanese: む、ムチャ言うなよ。
English: Only if I feel like dying!

Japanese: こっちには、ショウギの コマがあるね。《金将》だって。
English: Now, this yellow thing... This is a Japanese chess piece. I think it's a "king"?

Japanese: ま。あたしは五目ならべしか 知らないけどね。
English: Not that I'm an expert or anything... I'm more of a Reversi person, you know.

Japanese: （それはショウギですら ないぞ‥‥）
English: (Assuming she knows what she's talking about, these aren't exactly your typical...)
(..."mobster wannabe" items. They're not... "trophies", are they...? *gulp*)

Japanese: ‥‥あれ。
English: Hey...

Japanese: ‥‥なんか、ショウギのコマの 下から、紙キレがのぞいてるね。
English: There's a piece of paper sticking out from under here.

Japanese: なんだ、コレ。 ‥‥《修理代請求書》か‥‥。
English: What is it? ...A repair bill?

Japanese: （自動車の修理をしたらしいな）
English: (Looks like he did some repair work on his car... Does he even have one...?)

Japanese: バンパーとライトの交換、 １５０万円。‥‥高ェ！
English: $15,000 to replace a bumper and a light!? That's insane!

Japanese: クルマの持ち主は‥‥ 《シカバネ組》だって。
English: The car's registered to... the "Cadaverinis"?

Japanese: なんだ。ゼニトラじゃ ないのか。
English: Huh? So it's not even The Tiger's car?

Japanese: （他人のクルマの修理代請求書が、 どうしてこんなところに‥‥？）
English: (Why would someone else's repair bill be in The Tiger's office...?)

Japanese: 《修理代請求書》を 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Repair Bill added to the Court Record.

Japanese: ものすごいジュータンの上に、 ものすごいデスクが置かれている。
English: That's one impressive desk on one impressive rug!

Japanese: 黄金だよ、黄金！　やっぱり ちがうねー、カガヤキが。
English: It's solid gold, Nick! Gold! Just look at that shine! Only real gold shines like that.

Japanese: デスクにカガヤキが 必要なのかな‥‥。
English: Would you really want such a shiny desk, though?

Japanese: どれどれ。黄金の机の 使いごこちは、どうかな。
English: I don't know, but let's see what it's like to sit at a solid gold desk...

Japanese: ‥‥まっ、 まぶしいよ、なるほどくん！
English: Wow! I'm completely dazzled!

Japanese: そりゃ、まあ。黄金だからね。
English: That's because it's completely dazzling!

Japanese: それに、自分のハナの穴が 表面に写って、気が散るよ！
English: I can see up my nose in the reflection! That's gotta be really distracting!

Japanese: （使い勝手はよくないみたいだな。 ‥‥トーゼンだけど）
English: (So the desk isn't practical. No surprise there.)

Japanese: お。パリっぽいコートがあるよ。 オシャレさんだねー。
English: Hey, look at this Parisian-style coat! It's so chic!

Japanese: （ボクサーのガウンに見えるけど ‥‥気のせいだよな、モチロン）
English: (Looks more like a pimp coat to me... Guess I haven't got an eye for fashion.)

Japanese: ‥‥あれ。
English: Hey, look at this...

Japanese: このスーツ、なるほどくんが 着てるのと同じ色だね。
English: This suit is the same color as the one you wear, Nick.

Japanese: 同じ色の‥‥スーツ‥‥？
English: Hmm... The same color as my suit...?

Japanese: うわああああああっ！
English: Aaaaaaaah!

Japanese: お‥‥大きな声、出すなよ！
English: Keep your voice down, Maya!

Japanese: ななな、なるほどくん！ これ‥‥、ちょっと見てよ
English: N-Nick! You've got to take a look at this!

Japanese: おせんべい‥‥
English: Some cake...?

Japanese: きゃわわああああっ！
English: Aaaaaaaah!

Japanese: 置いておきますから、ここ‥‥
English: I'll just leave it here for you...

Japanese: あああ、あのあのあの！ あの、あたし‥‥！
English: Uh... yeah... sure... I, umm... Thanks...

Japanese: おとなしく待っててくださいね‥‥ でないと、わたし‥‥
English: Just wait here quietly... Otherwise...

Japanese: は、は、はい！
English: S-S-Sure!

Japanese: なるほどくん！　ダメだよ おとなしくしてなきゃ！
English: Did you hear that, Nick? Wait quietly, she said!

Japanese: え！　は、はあ。
English: Y-Yeah! Sure!

Japanese: ‥‥わたし‥‥見てますから‥‥。
English: I have my eye on you... Only so I can "take care of you"... Understand...?

Japanese: うううう‥‥コワいよお。
English: Aah! I'm scared, Nick!

Japanese: ‥‥‥で？　さっき、 何か見つけなかった？
English: ...So? What were you getting so excited about before?

Japanese: あのね。‥‥スーツのエリに‥‥
English: Look, on the lapel of this suit...

Japanese: あ‥‥ッ！　こ、これは‥‥ 弁護士バッジ‥‥？
English: That's...! That's an attorney's badge!

Japanese: ゼニトラって、 弁護士なのかな‥‥？
English: Is The Tiger a lawyer!?

Japanese: い、いやいや！　このバッジ、 ボール紙でできてるじゃないか！
English: No way! Look at this badge! It's made of paper!

Japanese: 《紙のバッジ》のデータを 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Paper Badge added to the Court Record.

Japanese: なんか、なるほどくんのバッジが 急に安っぽく見えてきたよ。
English: For some reason, your badge suddenly looks really cheap to me, Nick.

Japanese: （ダレか気づけよ！ こんなヒドいニセモノ‥‥）
English: (Why doesn't anyone recognize an obviously fake badge when they see one...?)

Japanese: あの‥‥。 この会社のことなんですけど。
English: So... I'm kind of curious about your company, Tender Lender...

Japanese: ‥‥カリヨーゼは、 中小企業のミカタ‥‥
English: ...With the warm and friendly atmosphere you'd expect from a family-sized business...

Japanese: 良心的な金利と、返済方法‥‥ みなさん、とてもよろこんで‥‥
English: ...a conscientious rate of interest... and an attractive repayment policy...

Japanese: （‥‥語尾が聞きとりにくいのが ちょっと気になるな）
English: (Why do I get the feeling this sentence is not going to end well...?)

Japanese: 闇金融業界の中で、他よりほんの 少し、明るめのカリヨーゼ‥‥
English: ...we will tenderly lend you that little bit extra, here at Tender Lender...

Japanese: そういえば、ウチの事務所も今、 ちょっと苦しいよね。
English: Hey, Nick. Things are a bit tight for Wright and Co. at the moment, aren't they?

Japanese: トランプの勝負で、なるほどくん、 あたしに５万円の借りがあるし。
English: I mean, there's that 500 dollars you owe me from our card game for starters.

Japanese: ホラ、なるほどくん。 借りる借りる。
English: Why don't you take out a loan?

Japanese: （だだ、だいじょうぶかなあ‥‥）
English: (Would I like to take out a loan from a place like this? Not so much...)

Japanese: ‥‥カリヨーゼは、そんな アナタのミカタ‥‥ククッ‥‥。
English: Tender Lender is... on your side... Hee... Hee... Hee...

Japanese: あの。もし返すのが遅れたら、 どうなるんですか？
English: So, um, let's say I'm late with my repayment... What happens then?

Japanese: ‥‥とびきり濃いお茶と‥‥ ‥‥‥‥‥を飲んでもらいます‥‥
English: ...We'd give you more coffee... ...Strong coffee...

Japanese: よそう。借金、踏み倒した ほうがマシだ。
English: Um, right... I think I'd rather skip town!

Japanese: あたしのお茶も濃いからね！
English: Hey! Just remember I can make strong coffee too, Nick! Strong tea, as well!

Japanese: ええと‥‥事件のことは、 ご存じですか？
English: So, um, do you know about the incident we're investigating?
What incident...?

Japanese: レストランで、男の人が 毒のコーヒーを飲んだ、アレです。
English: Well, a man was poisoned in a restaurant just near here. That incident.
Let me see...

Japanese: ‥‥その日は、店長と お店にいましたから‥‥
English: I was here that day, with the manager...

Japanese: 店長‥‥って、ゼニトラさんの ことですか？
English: The manager being The Tiger...?

Japanese: ‥‥芝九蔵　虎ノ助（しばくぞう とらのすけ）‥‥
English: ...Furio Tigre...

Japanese: し、しばくぞう‥‥
English: F-Furio...

Japanese: と、とらのすけ‥‥ （《ナニワのゼニトラ》‥‥か）
English: ...T-Tigre? (So that's where the "Tiger" thing comes from...)

Japanese: なるほどくんのニセモノだよ！ もっと、ハナシを聞こうよ！
English: Xin Eohp's got a real name! Nick! Hurry up and find out more about him!

Japanese: ‥‥わたし、もともとカラダが 弱いものですから‥‥
English: I'm very... frail, you see...

Japanese: ‥‥死んでしまいましたの‥‥ 一度。‥‥ククッ。
English: Just recently, I... died... Once... Hee... Hee...

Japanese: しし、死んだ‥‥？
English: You d-died!?

Japanese: ‥‥今から、４ヶ月ほど前‥‥
English: About 4 months ago...

Japanese: ‥‥もう絶望的だろう、って お医者さまが‥‥
English: The doctors said to... abandon all hope...

Japanese: （このヒトが“ゼツボー”って 言うと、シャレにならないな）
English: (I guess they were expecting her to take a boat ride across the River Styx.)

Japanese: それなのに‥‥
English: But Don Tigre...

Japanese: トラさまが、助けてくれた‥‥ すべてを投げうって‥‥
English: He saved me. He gave up everything...

Japanese: す、“すべてを”‥‥？
English: Everything!?

Japanese: うれしかった‥‥それで トラさまのキモチを知って‥‥
English: When I found out what he had done for me, I... was happy...

Japanese: ‥‥ちょっと、想像しにくいね。 ショージキなところ。
English: No offense, but I'm finding that a little hard to believe.

Japanese: このヒトのために、一生、お茶を いれてあげよう、って‥‥
English: I decided I'd pay him back... with my life... by serving him coffee... and espresso...

Japanese: （だいじょうぶなんだろうな。 そのお茶は‥‥）
English: (I still wonder about what's in her coffee...)

Japanese: あの‥‥ところで、 そのアタマのホータイは‥‥？
English: So... is that why you've got that bandage around your head?

Japanese: ククッ‥‥。これ、ですか‥‥
English: Hee... Hee... Hee... This?

Japanese: そのホータイ‥‥ どうしたんですか？
English: Um, so what's the story with the bandage?

Japanese: 手術のアト、なんです‥‥
English: They put it on after the operation...

Japanese: しゅじゅつ‥‥？
English: Operation?

Japanese: ちょっと‥‥致命傷を 負ってしまって‥‥ククッ‥‥。
English: It's just a little injury... A little, fatal, injury... Hee... Hee... Hee...

Japanese: ちちち、チメーショー！
English: A f-f-fatal injury!?

Japanese: （真宵ちゃん、ココロに 致命傷を負ったみたいだ‥‥）
English: (Maya just suffered one herself, by the sound of it...)

Japanese: さっき言ってた《一度、死んだ》 と言うのは、そのケガですか。
English: So, that's the injury you were talking about before when you said you had died once?

Japanese: ううう‥‥なんか、コワいよ なるほどくん。
English: Urgh! She really creeps me out, Nick!

Japanese: （でも‥‥聞き出さないワケにも いかない‥‥！）
English: (Same here. But we've got to find out the truth!)

Japanese: 手術を受けました‥‥ とても、ムズカシイ‥‥
English: It was very complicated... The operation...

Japanese: 脳の前頭葉部に、 大きな手術を‥‥
English: They opened up my head... And performed surgery on my frontal lobe...

Japanese: うううう‥‥コワいよお‥‥
English: Urgh! Just thinking about it sends shivers down my spine!

Japanese: その原因を作った 《致命傷》ですけど‥‥
English: So how did you get this "fatal injury" that left you needing an operation?

Japanese: ‥‥トラさまと ヤクソクしてますから‥‥
English: I made a promise to Don Tigre...

Japanese: “ダレにも言わない　ウソついたら お茶１００パイ、飲ーます”と。
English: I said I would never tell... Cross my heart and hope to drink 100 cups of espresso...

Japanese: ‥‥はあ。 （飲みたくないな、そりゃ‥‥）
English: Oh. (That much caffeine would raise anyone from the dead!)

Japanese: それに‥‥
English: Besides...

Japanese: 大切な“思い出”なんです‥‥
English: It's a very precious memory to me...

Japanese: お、おもいで‥‥ですか‥‥？ （解除するしかない、な‥‥）
English: A... A precious memory...? (I'll never find out what she means if I can't break her...)

Japanese: ４ヶ月前‥‥交通事故にあって 手術を受けた‥‥
English: Four months ago... I was in a traffic accident... That's why I needed the operation...

Japanese: わたし‥‥気づいてました‥‥。 たいした手術じゃない、って‥‥
English: When I woke up... they told me it was nothing serious... A simple procedure...

Japanese: え！　そ、そうだったんですか！
English: Oh! Really?

Japanese: （まあ、４ヶ月で治ってるぐらい だもんな‥‥）
English: (Well, I guess if she recovered in 4 months, it couldn't have been too big...)

Japanese: 手術代は‥‥１億円‥‥ そう聞きました‥‥
English: They said the operation... cost one million dollars...

Japanese: い‥‥い‥‥ いちおくえんンンッッッッ！ A... A...
English: A million bucks!?

Japanese: ‥‥おじいさまが。トラさまに 払うように、って‥‥
English: My grandfather ordered Don Tigre to pay...

Japanese: ‥‥１億円のほとんどは‥‥ “オトシマエ”だった‥‥
English: One million dollars... In "compensation"...

Japanese: なあに？　“オトシマエ”って‥‥
English: "Compensation", huh...?

Japanese: ウラの社会で、セキニンを とること、だよ。
English: It's underworld lingo for paying money to settle a score.

Japanese: ‥‥たいてい、タイヘンな 目にあうみたいだね。
English: Basically, pay or get into some serious trouble!

Japanese: （イチオクエン‥‥か）
English: (But a million bucks!?)

Japanese: （どうやら、つながったな。 今回の事件と‥‥）
English: (This has to be related to our poisoning case somehow...)

Japanese: ‥‥信じていたかった‥‥ トラさまのコトバ‥‥
English: I wanted to believe him... I wanted to trust what Don Tigre said...

Japanese: おじいさまは‥‥鹿羽　権太は いっさい、カンケイない。
English: He said it had nothing to do with my grandfather being Bruto Cadaverini...

Japanese: “わたしだから”助けてくれた。 そう思っていたかった‥‥
English: I wanted to believe he helped me because he cared about me. Not about my grandfather...

Japanese: そうじゃないことは‥‥ はじめから、わかっていたのに。
English: But I knew... That wasn't really true...

Japanese: う、うらみさん‥‥
English: Wow. I'm so sorry.

Japanese: トラさま‥‥１億円を払うために、 あんな、ひ‥‥ヒドいコトを‥‥
English: What he did to get the money was... It was... evil!

Japanese: “わたしのためだから”って‥‥ そう、自分に言い聞かせて‥‥
English: He said it was all for me... So I...

Japanese: だから、わたし‥‥ ‥‥手伝ってしまった‥‥
English: I helped him...

Japanese: て、“手伝った”‥‥？
English: You helped him? In what way?

Japanese: これ‥‥お持ちください。
English: Here... Take these...

Japanese: （これは‥‥診断書‥‥？）
English: (What are these? Medical papers?)

Japanese: わたし‥‥‥ 《鹿羽組》のムスメですから‥‥
English: I'm Bruto Cadaverini's granddaughter...

Japanese: オトシマエ‥‥つけなくては いけないんですよね‥‥
English: He had to pay compensation... He was made an offer... He simply... couldn't... refuse.

Japanese: 証拠品《うらみの診断書》を 法廷記録に挟んだ。
English: Viola's Medical Papers added to the Court Record.

Japanese: ‥‥かわいそうだね。 うらみさん。
English: Wow, I feel so bad for Viola.

Japanese: ユルせない。
English: It's inexcusable!
Huh?

Japanese: （ぼくには、どうしても ユルせないものが、２つある）
English: (There are two things that I consider inexcusable.)

Japanese: （“毒薬”と“ウラぎり”‥‥）
English: (Poisoning, and betrayal!)

Japanese: （最もヒキョウで、最も深く 人をキズつける‥‥）
English: (Only a coward would hurt people using either of these tactics.)

Japanese: な、なるほどくん‥‥？
English: Is everything alright, Nick?

Japanese: じゃあ‥‥ぼくたちも、そろそろ 行こうか。
English: We should get going.

Japanese: ‥‥このお茶を飲んだら。
English: Right after we finish our espresso.

Japanese: そうだね‥‥。
English: Yeah.

Japanese: （うらみさんに関する証拠は もう、いらないな‥‥）
English: (I won't need to convince Viola of anything else, so I guess I can get rid of this.)

Japanese: 《修理代請求書》を ゴミ箱に捨てた。
English: Repair Bill thrown into the trash.

Japanese: ゼニトラ‥‥芝九蔵さんのこと、 聞かせていただけませんか。
English: Can I ask you about The Tiger, I mean, Mr. Tigre?

Japanese: ‥‥ビスケット‥‥
English: ...Cookie...?

Japanese: は、はい？
English: S-Sure...?

Japanese: いかがですか‥‥。わたしが 焼いたんですけど‥‥ククッ‥‥。
English: How do you like my cookies? I baked them myself... Hee... Hee... Hee...

Japanese: なるほどくん、お先にどうぞ。
English: Go ahead, Nick! The honor is all yours!

Japanese: いやいや。真宵ちゃんこそ。
English: No, no! Ladies first, Maya!

Japanese: ‥‥ククッ。
English: Hee... Hee... Hee...

Japanese: （どうも、よくわからない。 どうしてゼニトラは‥‥）
English: (No matter how I look at this, I just don't get it...)

Japanese: （こんなコワいヒトと いっしょにいるんだろう‥‥？）
English: (What are The Tiger and this scary girl doing working together?)

Japanese: ‥‥わたしたち‥‥ 愛しあっていますから‥‥
English: We... are lovers...

Japanese: （そんな、うらめしげに 言われてもなあ‥‥）
English: (That's not exactly coming across in your tone of voice...)

Japanese: イノチの恩人なんです。 トラさま‥‥
English: And I owe Don Tigre my life... He's the one who saved me...

Japanese: あの、ゼニトラが‥‥？
English: The Tiger... saved you...!?

Japanese: “トラさま”とお呼びください‥‥ でないと、わたし‥‥
English: Please, address him properly as "Don Tigre". Otherwise, I'll have to...

Japanese: とと、トラさまですね、はい！
English: OK, OK! Don Tigre! Of course! I'm sorry.

Japanese: （イノチの恩人、ねえ‥‥ もう少し、話を聞いてみるか）
English: (He saved her life? I'd sure like to know how that happened.)

Japanese: このヒト‥‥あなたの おじいさん、なんですか？
English: This man is your grandfather, right?

Japanese: そんな写真‥‥持ち歩かない ほうがいいです‥‥
English: Carrying a picture like that around with you... You should watch your back...
Th-Thanks for the advice...

Japanese: ‥‥組のヒトが、いつもわたしを カゲから見てるから‥‥
English: The family is always watching me... from the shadows...

Japanese: （く、組のヒト‥‥？）
English: (The "family"?)

Japanese: あ、あのダルマの中が あやしいよ、なるほどくん！
English: I thought I saw something suspicious under the corner of that rug!

Japanese: ヘタに動くと‥‥ 消されますよ‥‥ククッ。
English: One wrong move and... you might disappear forever... Hee... Hee... Hee...

Japanese: （‥‥コワい世界だなあ‥‥）
English: (This feels like Halloween... Only a whole lot more real... And a whole lot scarier, too.)

Japanese: あの。これ、なんですけど‥‥。
English: Um, about this...

Japanese: ‥‥お茶‥‥ おかわり、いれてきますね‥‥。
English: More coffee...? You must have more...

Japanese: い、いえいえ！　ケッコウです！ （ノドはカラカラだけどな‥‥）
English: No! Um, thanks! I've had enough. Really! (I'm so thirsty!)

Japanese: わたし‥‥トラさましか 目に入りませんから‥‥ククッ。
English: I don't leave the office... I can't tell you about anything... except Don Tigre.

Japanese: ～アタマの包帯～
English: -- The Head Bandage --

Japanese: アタマの包帯‥‥ “手術のアト”なんですよね？
English: You said that bandage around your head was from an operation?

Japanese: 脳に“致命傷”を負った、とか。
English: You also said you suffered a "fatal injury" to the head, correct?

Japanese: ‥‥ええ‥‥ムズカシイ手術 だったそうです‥‥
English: Yes... The operation was very... difficult, apparently...

Japanese: “致命傷”ということは‥‥ “ケガ”をしたんですよね？
English: Now, by "fatal injury", you mean you were hurt very badly somehow, right?
Hee... Hee...

Japanese: “ケガ”の原因‥‥。この証拠品と 関係があるんじゃないですか？
English: Did the injury in question have something to do with this?

Japanese: どうですか！
English: Well!?

Japanese: おいなりさん‥‥。
English: Donuts...?

Japanese: は？
English: Huh?

Japanese: ‥‥わたし、にぎってきたんです。 ‥‥おいなりさん‥‥
English: I baked them myself... Home-made donuts...

Japanese: いただきませんか‥‥？
English: Have one...

Japanese: あの。ナニが入ってるんですか？
English: Umm, what's inside?
Jam, and...

Japanese: え？　よく聞こえません でしたけど。
English: I'm sorry, but I didn't quite catch that...
Hee... Hee...

Japanese: ‥‥い、いりません。 （シッパイみたいだな‥‥）
English: Um... Thanks, but no thanks... I think I'll pass. (I guess that was a flop.)

Japanese: （彼女がなぜ、アタマに それほど大きなケガをしたのか）
English: (What happened to this woman for her to have such a huge bandage around her head?)

Japanese: （その“原因”をニオわせる 証拠品、ないかな‥‥？）
English: (There must be some piece of evidence that'll prompt her to tell me what happened...)

Japanese: ここに、自動車の修理代の 請求書があります。
English: I have here a car repair bill.

Japanese: 当然、考えられるのは‥‥ 交通事故、ですよね。
English: From this, it seems pretty obvious that this car was involved in an accident.

Japanese: ‥‥その請求書‥‥ 見せてください‥‥
English: Let me see that...

Japanese: 修理代の受取人‥‥ 《鹿羽組》‥‥ですか‥‥
English: This bill is made out to the "Cadaverinis"...?

Japanese: そ、そうですね。
English: Y-Yes, it is.

Japanese: わたし‥‥自己紹介した おぼえ、ないのですけど‥‥
English: I don't think... I ever introduced myself...

Japanese: この《鹿羽組》とわたし‥‥ どういうカンケイなんですか？
English: Tell me... what do the "Cadaverinis"... have to do with me...?

Japanese: （やれやれ‥‥自己紹介して くれそうなフンイキじゃないな）
English: (Something tells me she's not about to say hi and introduce herself... Alright, then...)

Japanese: あなたと、《鹿羽組》の カンケイは‥‥
English: Your relationship with the Cadaverinis is very strong... And this is why.

Japanese: さあ、いかがですか！
English: So!? What do you have to say for yourself now!?

Japanese: たくあん‥‥なんですけど‥‥
English: Pretzels... For you...

Japanese: はい？
English: Huh?

Japanese: このたくあん‥‥ キレイな黄色でしょう‥‥？
English: Don't worry about the little white specks on the surface...

Japanese: この色を出すために‥‥ ‥‥‥‥‥を入れましたから‥‥。
English: I just sprinkled a small quantity of...

Japanese: ‥‥ケッコウです。 （シッパイか‥‥）
English: ...N-No thanks! (My evidence must still be half-baked...)

Japanese: ‥‥《鹿羽組》なんて知りません。 ‥‥そもそも、わたし‥‥
English: I really don't know... the "Cadaverinis"... Further...

Japanese: あなたのことは、わかっています。 ‥‥鹿羽　うらみさん。
English: I know exactly who you are, Viola Cadaverini!

Japanese: あなたの、そのケガ‥‥ 交通事故、だったんですね？
English: You sustained that injury in a traffic accident, didn't you?

Japanese: ４ヶ月ほど前のコトでした‥‥
English: It happened about 4 months ago...

Japanese: 組のクルマの前に、いきなり 飛び出してきた人がいました‥‥
English: I was driving in one of our family's cars... when someone pulled out in front of me...

Japanese: オートバイ、だったような‥‥ よく、おぼえていません‥‥
English: It was a motorbike... or something like that... I don't remember it much...

Japanese: そのオートバイと接触して、 ハンドルを切りそこねて‥‥
English: Anyway, I swerved to try to avoid it, but...

Japanese: ‥‥わたし、打ちどころが 悪かったそうです。‥‥異常に。
English: ...I took a blow... to the head... A bad one.

Japanese: （‥‥なんか、 わかる気がするな‥‥）
English: (Yeah, I can imagine...)

Japanese: それで‥‥その、飛び出してきた 人は、どうなったんですか？
English: So what happened to the person on the bike?

Japanese: タダじゃすまなかったと 思いますけど‥‥。
English: I'm guessing they didn't get away with injuring THE Viola Cadaverini, right?

Japanese: ‥‥わたし、知りません‥‥
English: I... don't know... what happened to them...

Japanese: ‥‥そのまま逃げてしまった、 と聞いています‥‥
English: They ran away... Or so I heard...

Japanese: 逃げた‥‥
English: Ran away?

Japanese: そうでなければ、わたし‥‥ ククッ‥‥。
English: If they'd stayed, I'd have... Hee... Hee... Hee...

Japanese: （もしかして‥‥その、 “逃げた人”というのは‥‥）
English: (Hmm, is it possible...? Could the person who committed the hit and run have been...?)

Japanese: あなたの事故の原因を 作ったのは、もしかして‥‥
English: The person who caused the accident that gave you that injury was--

Japanese: ‥‥もう、いいんです‥‥。 考えたくないから‥‥
English: No more... I don't want to... think about it anymore...

Japanese: ‥‥それより、トマト‥‥ うちの組でとれたんですけど‥‥
English: Here... try one of these tomatoes...

Japanese: わたしが調合した薬が たっぷりと‥‥ククッ‥‥。
English: We all picked them together... as a family... And I added my own, special, flavoring...

Japanese: た、食べません。
English: N-No thanks!

Japanese: （交通事故の原因を作った人物は、 “オートバイに乗っていた”）
English: (The person who caused the accident was riding a motorbike... Who could it be?)

Japanese: ‥‥事故の原因を作ったのは、 このオトコ‥‥じゃないですか？
English: It was this man, wasn't it? He was the cause of your accident.

Japanese: と‥‥トラさまじゃない！ わたし、信じません‥‥
English: It wasn't Don Tigre! I refuse to believe it!

Japanese: 相手のオートバイ‥‥ わたしのクルマと接触しました‥‥
English: We collided... The motorbike, and my car...

Japanese: でも、トラさまは‥‥ケガなど していませんでしょう‥‥？
English: But Don Tigre isn't injured at all... is he?

Japanese: （ゼニトラは、鹿羽組の クルマに接触したんだ‥‥）
English: (It was The Tiger who caused Viola to crash. I can feel it!)

Japanese: （ロックが割れたということは‥‥ 彼女もそれを疑っている‥‥！）
English: (Plus, one of her locks just broke, so she must suspect it was him, too...)

Japanese: ‥‥ザンネンですが‥‥
English: I'm sorry, Ms. Cadaverini...

Japanese: 彼が“交通事故にあった”という 可能性を示す証拠があります！
English: But I have proof that The Tiger was involved in a traffic accident on his bike!

Japanese: これが、その証拠ですッ！
English: And this is my proof!

Japanese: ‥‥ゆでたまご‥‥ おひとつ、いかがですか‥‥
English: Hard-boiled eggs...? Please, try one...

Japanese: た、たまご‥‥？
English: E-Eggs?

Japanese: わたしが、大切に育てた ニワトリがうんだタマゴ‥‥
English: I raised the chickens these eggs came from myself...

Japanese: 青いヒヨコをうむように、 トクベツなエサを配合して‥‥
English: I invented a special diet... so their chicks would be blue...

Japanese: ややや、やめときます。
English: N-No, thanks!

Japanese: ‥‥“オートバイ”では ありませんが‥‥
English: It's not exactly a motorbike, but...

Japanese: 芝九蔵さん、スクーターを 乗り回していますよね。
English: ...Mr. Tigre rides around on a scooter, doesn't he?

Japanese: 前のカバーが、ハデに コワれています。
English: And you'll notice that the front wheel guard is badly damaged.

Japanese: うらみさん‥‥
English: Ms. Cadaverini...

Japanese: ホントは、あなたも 気づいているのでは‥‥？
English: You know the truth, don't you?
Hee... Hee... Hee...

Japanese: この修理代請求書‥‥支払人の名は 芝九蔵　虎ノ助です。
English: This repair bill was paid by Furio Tigre.

Japanese: 《鹿羽組》は、事故のハンニンを、 とっくに見つけていたんですよ。
English: The Cadaverinis have known for ages who caused the accident, haven't they?

Japanese: ‥‥もしかしたら‥‥ そうかもしれない‥‥
English: It's possible... Perhaps...

Japanese: ‥‥ココロのスミっこで、 そう思っていました‥‥
English: Somewhere inside me... I know that may be true...

Japanese: （やっぱり‥‥）
English: (I knew it...)
But...

Japanese: トラさまは、わたしの イノチを助けてくれた‥‥
English: Don Tigre still saved my life...

Japanese: 手術はとてもむずかしくて‥‥ おカネがかかりました‥‥
English: The operation was very complicated... It was very, very expensive...

Japanese: いったい、いくらぐらい‥‥？
English: How much are we talking?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥とても。 とても、たくさん‥‥。
English: Very, very, very expensive...

Japanese: （‥‥どうやら、金額は 言いたくないみたいだな‥‥）
English: (She seems kind of hesitant about giving me an actual figure. I should back off...)

Japanese: それを支払ったのが‥‥ ゼニトラ、というわけですか。
English: Well, anyway, it was The Tiger who paid for it, right?

Japanese: ‥‥なんとか助かってから‥‥ トラさまが、言ってくれました‥‥
English: After I recovered... Don Tigre told me...

Japanese: “うらみちゃんだから” おカネを出した、って‥‥
English: He said he paid for the operation because... he cared about me...

Japanese: わたし‥‥それを 信じてますから‥‥。
English: I... believe him...

Japanese: ‥‥本当に‥‥ そうだったんでしょうか。
English: Really? But do you honestly believe that to be true?

Japanese: もしかしたら‥‥
English: Do you want to know what I think...?

Japanese: 芝九蔵さんが手術代を出したのは、 あなたのためではなく‥‥
English: I think the reason he paid for the operation wasn't because of you, but someone else...

Japanese: ‥‥あなたには、ショックだと 思います。しかし‥‥！
English: I know this must be quite a shock for you, but...

Japanese: ちゃんちゃんこ‥‥
English: Overcoat...?

Japanese: は、はい‥‥？
English: S-Sorry?

Japanese: わたし、作りました‥‥ ‥‥最近、とてもサムいから‥‥
English: I made it myself... It's been so... cold lately...

Japanese: （この声を聞いてるほうが ココロまで冷えこむぞ‥‥）
English: (Yeah, just hearing your voice sends shivers down my spine!)

Japanese: どうぞ。着てください。 ‥‥‥‥あ‥‥‥‥
English: Please, try it on... ...Oops!

Japanese: 今の“あ”は、なんですか？
English: What was the "oops" for?

Japanese: ‥‥マチバリ‥‥足りない‥‥
English: I'm missing a few pins... I must have left them in the coat...

Japanese: や、やっぱり エンリョしておきます！
English: O-On second thought, I'm fine, thanks! Roasty-toasty, that's me!

Japanese: トラさま‥‥わたしに 言ってくれたんです‥‥
English: Don Tigre told me, you see...

Japanese: ‥‥こんなコト、言っちゃ いけないのかもしれませんけど‥‥
English: Perhaps I shouldn't be saying this, but...

Japanese: おじいさんの鹿羽　権太さん、 闇金融のボス、なんですよね？
English: ...your grandfather, Bruto Cadaverini, controls a lot of "dubious" cash, right?

Japanese: そして、マゴのあなたを 生きがいにしている。
English: And you are his beloved pride and joy.

Japanese: その手術代の金額にも よりますけど‥‥、
English: Sure, I don't know exactly how much the operation cost, but...

Japanese: もし、あなたが鹿羽　権太の マゴでなかったら‥‥
English: ...if you weren't the granddaughter of Mr. Cadaverini...

Japanese: 芝九蔵さんは、その カネを出したでしょうか‥‥？
English: ...do you think Mr. Tigre would have paid the money?

Japanese: ‥‥いちおくえん‥‥
English: One million dollars...

Japanese: （今はまだ、証拠が 足りないのかもしれない）
English: (I don't think I have enough evidence yet...)

Japanese: （もう少し調査をして、 材料を集めてみるか‥‥）
English: (I should investigate and gather some more clues before I try again...)

Japanese: ‥なるほどくん‥
English: ...Mr. Nick...

Japanese: ‥これ以上つづけると、 ココロがこわれてしまいます‥
English: ...If you push yourself any more, your soul will shatter...

Japanese: ‥もう一度、冷静になって 考えなおしましょう‥
English: ...Please calm down, collect your thoughts, and try again...

Japanese: （‥‥しまった！ シッパイしすぎたか‥‥）
English: (Nnrgh! I've made too many mistakes!)
